rationale: The citing paper uses Ailawadi et al. (2010) as a key prior empirical benchmark on Wal-Mart entry effects, summarizing its findings on incumbent revenue losses and assortment-based mitigation in the literature review. It then reuses that specific insight to motivate its moderator analysis of incumbent assortment size and overlap, and later states its own results are "extending results reported in Ailawadi et al. (2010)." Although Ailawadi et al. is also cited once in a grouped methods reference for DID, the main role is conceptual and empirical positioning that informs hypothesis interpretation, not a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing study extends Ailawadi et al. (2010) from incumbent retailer sales outcomes to supplier-level profits and shipments after Wal-Mart entry. It specifically carries forward and broadens the assortment-mitigation logic by showing incumbent assortment breadth and lower overlap with Wal-Mart are linked to better supplier outcomes.
